title: grisebachia eriocephala
intro: Grisebachia eriocephala, commonly known as the New Zealand helmet orchid, is a rare and threatened flowering plant native to New Zealand. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, with its roots and leaves being used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Grisebachia eriocephala include its ability to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as its potential to improve respiratory function and boost the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made by drying and processing the roots and leaves, which can then be used in teas, tinctures, and infusions to harness its medicinal properties.
